trigger: Rapid in-out movement on a recently opened low-activity account

triage.explanation: The account shows a pass-through pattern: a large inbound credit from a company quickly followed by an outbound debit of nearly identical amount to a money changer within hours, leaving a nominal balance. The account is recently opened, consistent with PT-01 indicators.

matchedTypology.name: Pass-through / Rapid Movement
matchedTypology.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10/R.20; BNM AML/CFT Policy Document

indicators: Inbound credit followed by outbound debit of a similar amount within a short window (minutes to a few days)
runningBalance spikes on the inbound then drains back to ~0
Little to no funds retained; no spending consistent with the stated purpose of the account
Often a newly opened or recently dormant account
fired: Inbound credit followed by outbound debit of a similar amount within a short window (minutes to a few days)
runningBalance spikes on the inbound then drains back to ~0
Little to no funds retained; no spending consistent with the stated purpose of the account
Often a newly opened or recently dormant account

verifier.note: The personal account shows irregular timing, diverse counterparties, and no commercial rationale, matching pass-through laundering rather than a benign sweep.

typology.name: Pass-through / Rapid Movement
typology.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10/R.20; BNM AML/CFT Policy Document

strDraft.activitySummary: On 14 June 2026, Tan Wei Ming's account received an inbound credit of MYR 52,000 from Evergreen Logistics Ltd, followed within 2 hours by an outbound debit of MYR 51,500 to MaxCash Money Changer. The running balance spiked to MYR 52,600 and then drained to MYR 1,100, indicating no meaningful retention of funds. The account activity shows a rapid pass-through with no apparent economic purpose or spending consistent with the stated account purpose.
groundsForSuspicion: Inbound credit of MYR 52,000 from Evergreen Logistics Ltd followed within 2 hours by an outbound debit of MYR 51,500 to MaxCash Money Changer.
Running balance spiked briefly to MYR 52,600 before dropping to near-zero (MYR 1,100), showing no retention of funds.
The account is recently opened and exhibits a pass-through pattern with no economic justification or legitimate spending.
The near-identical amounts and rapid movement are indicative of layering through a money changer, consistent with money laundering typologies.
strDraft.recommendedAction: Escalate to the Financial Intelligence and Enforcement Department (FIED).

typology.name: Pass-through / Rapid Movement

strDraft.activitySummary: On 14 June 2026, Kopitiam Aroma Sdn Bhd received two POS settlement credits totaling 6,000 MYR within two minutes, spiking the balance to 8,200 MYR. Within two hours, a 5,500 MYR transfer was made to the company's operating account, draining the balance to 2,700 MYR. The rapid movement of nearly all incoming funds with no apparent economic rationale is indicative of a pass-through scheme.

citedTransactions.counterpartyName: Kopitiam Aroma Sdn Bhd - Operating A/C

groundsForSuspicion: Inbound POS settlements totalling MYR 6,000 were followed within two hours by an outbound transfer of MYR 5,500, consistent with rapid pass-through.
Running balance spiked from MYR 2,200 to MYR 8,200 and then dropped to MYR 2,700, leaving only a small residual.
The transaction pattern lacks clear economic purpose, as a kopitiam would not typically move POS receipts immediately to an operating account in a single lump sum.
strDraft.recommendedAction: Escalate to the Financial Intelligence and Enforcement Department (FIED).

trigger: Account dormant ~16 months then sudden high-value throughput; no CDD refresh on file

triage.explanation: Account dormant for ~16 months, then large inbound followed immediately by similar outbound, draining the balance, with no CDD refresh, matching all DA-01 indicators.

matchedTypology.name: Dormant-then-Active

groundsForSuspicion: Account dormant for ~16 months before sudden reactivation.

Activity profile contradicts historical low/no activity pattern.

trigger: Repeated cash deposits just below the RM25,000 CTR threshold across multiple branches and days

triage.explanation: Three cash deposits (24200, 23800, 24100 MYR) occur just below the RM25,000 threshold across three consecutive days and at three different branches, aggregating to over RM72,000 before a rapid outward transfer. This matches the structuring/smurfing typology (ST-01) perfectly.

matchedTypology.name: Structuring / Smurfing
matchedTypology.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.20; BNM AMLA 2001; CTR threshold RM25,000

indicators: Multiple CASH deposits clustered just under the RM25,000 CTR threshold
Repeated similar sub-threshold amounts over a short period
Spread across DAYS, BRANCHES, or multiple PERSONS (smurfing) to defeat same-day aggregation
Total aggregated value is substantial
fired: Multiple CASH deposits clustered just under the RM25,000 CTR threshold
Repeated similar sub-threshold amounts over a short period
Spread across DAYS, BRANCHES, or multiple PERSONS (smurfing) to defeat same-day aggregation
Total aggregated value is substantial

verifier.note: Cash deposits just below RM25,000 across different branches on consecutive days with no documented commercial reason fit structuring, not benign recurring payments.

typology.name: Structuring / Smurfing
typology.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.20; BNM AMLA 2001; CTR threshold RM25,000

strDraft.activitySummary: Over three consecutive days (10–12 June 2026), Daniel Choo Kah Wai deposited RM24,200, RM23,800, and RM24,100 in cash at three different branches—each amount deliberately just below the RM25,000 CTR threshold—aggregating to RM72,100 before a same-day outward transfer of RM70,000 to Sterling Overseas Trading, leaving a minimal balance.
groundsForSuspicion: Three cash deposits (RM24,200, RM23,800, RM24,100) all within RM1,200 of the RM25,000 reporting threshold
Deposits made on three consecutive days at three different branches (Bukit Bintang, Cheras, Petaling Jaya) to evade same-day aggregation
Total cash introduced (RM72,100) is substantial and rapidly transferred out, inconsistent with normal account activity
No apparent economic rationale for multiple just-below-threshold cash deposits followed by an immediate large transfer
strDraft.recommendedAction: Escalate to the Financial Intelligence and Enforcement Department (FIED).

subject.holderName: Nurul Aina binti Hamzah (declared: admin clerk, income RM3,000/mo)

typology.name: KYC Profile Mismatch
typology.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10 (CDD); BNM AML/CFT Policy Document

strDraft.activitySummary: The account, held by an admin clerk with a declared monthly income of RM3,000, received RM150,000 from a Dubai-based company on 12 June 2026, followed within hours by outbound transfers of RM96,000 to a Cayman Islands entity and RM47,000 to an individual. The total throughput in one day exceeded 50 times the customer’s expected monthly income and involved international counterparties entirely inconsistent with the personal account profile.
groundsForSuspicion: Throughput of RM150,000 in a single day far exceeds the declared monthly income of RM3,000, representing a disproportionate activity level for the customer profile.
The account is a basic personal account but handled high-value international wires, which is inconsistent with its intended purpose.
Counterparties in Dubai and the Cayman Islands, along with cross-border fund movements, are not aligned with the customer’s domestic, low-income occupation.
The stated occupation as an admin clerk does not explain the receipt of corporate funds or the onward transmission to an offshore entity.
No CDD refresh is on file to support the sudden and significant change in account behaviour.
strDraft.recommendedAction: Escalate to the Financial Intelligence and Enforcement Department (FIED).
